# Daily Report — 2026-06-25

## Task Completed
Added `update_change_schedule` tool (PATCH cmn_schedule/{sys_id}).  
`UpdateChangeScheduleParams` accepts a required `schedule_id` (sys_id or exact name) plus six optional fields: `name`, `schedule_type`, `time_zone`, `active`, `parent`, and `description`.  
Key behaviours:
- `schedule_id` resolved via `_resolve_change_schedule_sys_id` (32-char hex passthrough or name-based GET lookup)
- `parent` also resolved via the same helper when provided as a name
- Empty-body guard returns failure before any PATCH is sent
- `active` serialised as `"true"`/`"false"` string to match ServiceNow API convention
- 404 guard on PATCH response
- Registered in `change_coordinator` and `full` tool packages

## Test Results
- Total tests: 1829
- Coverage: 93%
- All passing: yes

## Security
Pre-existing system-level findings only (setuptools, urllib3, wheel) — not introduced by this change.

- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — imported params class and registered tool in registry
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — added to `change_coordinator` and `full` packages
- `tests/test_update_change_schedule.py` — 21 new tests (happy path, empty-body guard, 404, name resolver, parent resolver, connection errors)

## Next Up
Add delete_change_schedule tool (DELETE cmn_schedule/{sys_id}; 204/404 guards)
